metadata.from: Bob Hanifen — writing to his father, describing arrival at a military base/training location
metadata.location_written_from: Military barracks, likely a training base in Texas or the South (passed through Tulsa, Dallas, Ft. Worth area)
topics: train journey through Tulsa and Dallas
metadata.mood_tone: Upbeat and descriptive; writer is adjusting to new surroundings and sharing details of the journey and living conditions with enthusiasm
metadata.legibility_notes: Both pages are heavily smeared/bleed-through from ink on reverse side, making large portions very difficult to read. Many words and phrases are partially or wholly illegible. Page 2 is especially difficult due to severe ink bleed-through overlapping the writing.
metadata.historical_context: Letter appears to be from WWII era military service; Bob is describing his arrival at a military installation, traveling by train through the Midwest/South. References to barracks housing 48 men (expanded to 94) suggest wartime overcrowding.
metadata.date_estimation_reasoning: Bob describes arriving at a new military base after traveling from Leavenworth through Tulsa and Dallas/Ft. Worth. Letter 46 (postmarked April 15, 1945) was written from Fort Leavenworth and mentions shipping to Texas on Monday. Letter 21 (April 23, 1945 postmark) is from Sheppard Field, Texas. This letter describes arriving at a base after the Leavenworth-to-Texas journey, placing it between those two letters. The flooding referenced ('Flood water held us up all day') matches spring 1945 conditions. The barracks 'made for 48 men which has now a total of 94 men' is consistent with overcrowded wartime training bases. Estimated approximately April 18–21, 1945.

pages.transcription:
Dear Dad

[illegible line at top]

We are here at last. Got here Wed Morn. We left Leavenworth Monday night at 5:30 next morning were in Tulsa. Flood water held us up all day and night. Made us go through Tulsa instead of Oklahoma City. The water was over the tracks in some places and all the ground around was really covered. Got to Dallas that night and then to Ft. Worth, went to bed and they let us sleep that night and took us to the field in the morning. Had a good breakfast and am now in my new home a barracks made for 48 men which has now a total of 94 men. We get no foot locker and [I] don't know what to do with all my stuff. Every body else has the same trouble. [illegible] got the best [?] base in here and [illegible] who [k]nows. This is [a] nice [looking?] place. Mess Hall [illegible] nice the place. We get up at 5:15 [and] take off for the day at 6:00 not too bad. Had a swell [illegible] just a few minutes ago.

We had steak and when [illegible]
I mean steak [—] a real good [juicy?] piece as big as [illegible] [paper?] and [illegible]

full_transcription:
Dear Dad

[illegible line at top]

We are here at last. Got here Wed Morn. We left Leavenworth Monday night at 5:30 next morning were in Tulsa. Flood water held us up all day and night. Made us go through Tulsa instead of Oklahoma City. The water was over the tracks in some places and all the ground around was really covered. Got to Dallas that night and then to Ft. Worth, went to bed and they let us sleep that night and took us to the field in the morning. Had a good breakfast and am now in my new home a barracks made for 48 men which has now a total of 94 men. We get no foot locker and [I] don't know what to do with all my stuff. Every body else has the same trouble. [illegible] got the best [?] base in here and [illegible] who [k]nows. This is [a] nice [looking?] place. Mess Hall [illegible] nice the place. We get up at 5:15 [and] take off for the day at 6:00 not too bad. Had a swell [illegible] just a few minutes ago.

We had steak and when [illegible] I mean steak [—] a real good [juicy?] piece as big as [illegible] and [illegible] mashed [potatoes?], [salad,] [coffee,] [illegible] no [milk?] [illegible] [already?] [getting?] hungry [—] it was after a [good?] dinner [ourselves?] [illegible] [to eat?] [Well?] [illegible] now [illegible] [move?] to a [new unit?] [list?]. It is cloudy again today but [it] should be getting [fine?] [—] [instead?], [Picked?] [illegible] [—] in Leavenworth [—] the [illegible] the [effect?] [illegible] [bring?] me [right?] [hopped?] up [but?] [illegible] [—] too bad [for?] [us?] [illegible] We [get?] C [illegible] [—] [that's?] [my?] [—] [take?] [a?] [little?] [—] [illegible] [Des?] Moines [—] [if?] [Dad?] [has?] [been?] [and?] [illegible] [—] [it?] [will?] [me?] [—] [at?] [top?] [—] [granted?] [for?] [more?] [—] [that?] [it?] [had?] [—] [wound?] [near?] [—] [and?] [me?] [—] [that?] [—] [those?] [—] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible — closing and signature]
summary: Bob writes to his father describing his arrival at a new military base after a train journey from Leavenworth through Tulsa (detoured due to flooding) and Dallas/Ft. Worth. He describes his crowded barracks (designed for 48 men but housing 94), the lack of foot lockers, the early morning schedule (up at 5:15, off at 6:00), and a good steak dinner at the mess hall. Page 2 is heavily obscured by ink bleed-through, making the latter portion of the letter largely illegible, though references to Des Moines and general camp life are faintly visible.
